full_prompt: Design the primary driver-cell monocoque and roll-cage structure of a Challenger-class cross-country solar-EV benchmark targeting the 6 m^2 silicon array sub-class. Vehicle overall length must not exceed 5 m, width must not exceed 2.2 m, height must not exceed 1.8 m per road-law annex. Total vehicle mass excluding driver must not exceed 500 kg. The solar array footprint must be <= 6.0 m^2 projected horizontal area of silicon cells. Roll cage is mandatory per cross-country solar-EV standard, constructed from 4130 chromoly steel tubing (Young's modulus 205 GPa, Poisson 0.29, yield 435 MPa, ultimate 670 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3), minimum outer diameter 31.75 mm x wall 1.65 mm, with a main hoop over the driver's head providing at least 50 mm clearance above the helmet. Driver cell monocoque is carbon/epoxy sandwich with quasi-isotropic 1.5 mm CFRP skins (E = 55 GPa, strength 600 MPa), 20 mm aluminum honeycomb core (E3 = 0.8 GPa, shear strength 1.2 MPa, density 50 kg/m^3). Load cases (cross-country solar-EV structural inheritance): (LC1) main hoop vertical 6 kN + lateral 5 kN + longitudinal 9 kN simultaneously at the top of the hoop with base fixed; (LC2) front bulkhead: 6000 N axial applied at the bulkhead centerline representing front-impact equivalent load; (LC3) side intrusion: 7000 N normal to the side-impact structure at mid-height; (LC4) harness/belt anchor: 13,500 N at each of the 5-point harness anchors per FIA 8854/98-equivalent at the most adverse angle; (LC5) roof solar array downforce + aerodynamic lift: aero lift 800 N uniformly distributed over the 6 m^2 array footprint at 100 km/h race speed. Pass/fail: (R1) no tube or laminate element exceeds yield/first-ply-failure across LC1-LC4 (435 MPa steel, 600 MPa CFRP), (R2) deflection of main hoop top toward helmet under LC1 below 50 mm, (R3) CFRP skin strain under LC5 below 0.4%, (R4) core shear below 0.8 MPa under LC5, (R5) first global torsional mode above 10 Hz. Deliverables: STEP, layup table, FEA per case, modal report, and full mass budget separating cage/skin/core/array.
short_prompt:
Design the driver-cell monocoque and roll cage of a Challenger-class cross-country solar-EV targeting the 6 m^2 silicon array sub-class. The cage is 4130 chromoly steel tubing; the driver cell is a carbon/epoxy sandwich with CFRP skins and aluminum honeycomb core. Qualify to the cross-country solar-EV structural inheritance (tube gates from the main hoop + side + bulkhead + harness set, with FIA 8854/98-equivalent harness load), targeting yield/first-ply-failure.

Envelope: vehicle L <= 5 m, W <= 2.2 m, H <= 1.8 m; vehicle mass (no driver) <= 500 kg; PV array <= 6.0 m^2 projected; cage tube OD >= 31.75 mm, wall >= 1.65 mm, main hoop >= 50 mm above helmet.

Load cases.
- LC1 main hoop: 6 kN vertical + 5 kN lateral + 9 kN longitudinal simultaneous at the top of the hoop, base fixed.
- LC2 front bulkhead: 6000 N axial at the bulkhead centerline.
- LC3 side intrusion: 7000 N normal to the side-impact structure at mid-height.
- LC4 harness anchors: 13,500 N at each of five 5-point harness anchors in the most adverse direction.
- LC5 aero lift: 800 N up as UDL over the 6 m^2 array at 100 km/h.

prompt.title: Challenger-class solar EV monocoque + roll cage
prompt.brief: Challenger-class 6 m^2 solar EV with cross-country inherited cage and CFRP sandwich monocoque.
